name: Nebius Group N.V.

profile.description: Nebius Group N.V. is a full-stack AI cloud infrastructure company that builds large-scale GPU clusters and operates an AI-native cloud platform for training, deploying, and running AI models. Rebranded from Yandex N.V. after selling $5.4B in Russian assets in 2024, the company now operates data centers across Europe and North America. Q1 2026 revenue reached $399M (+684% YoY) with core AI cloud ARR of $1.92B. Backed by a cumulative $6.3B in capital including NVIDIA equity investment and convertible notes, Nebius holds a $12B+ infrastructure agreement with Meta Platforms and is guiding for $3.0-$3.4B in 2026 revenue.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (3/3): The transformation from Yandex N.V. — Russia's dominant internet company — into a pure-play Western AI cloud provider within 18 months is one of the most dramatic corporate reinventions in tech history; Q1 2026 revenue of $399M grew 684% YoY, with core AI cloud ARR surging 54% QoQ to $1.92B [1]. AI Deployment (2/2): Nebius IS the AI product — operating GPU clusters with NVIDIA B300/GB300 NVL72 systems for AI training and inference, with the Tavily agentic search acquisition and Robotics Simulation cloud demonstrating platform breadth [2]. Market Share (1/2): Nebius is the fastest-growing AI neocloud in Europe and among the top globally, but is not yet a category leader against hyperscalers — Meta's $12B+ agreement and NVIDIA's preferred provider status validate market position [2]. Pipeline (1/2): Priority access to NVIDIA Vera Rubin platform (deploying in 2026), 1.2 GW Pennsylvania AI factory site secured, and Meta capacity starting early 2027 provide concrete pipeline visibility [1][2]. R&D Intensity (1/1): CapEx guidance increased to $20-25B for 2026 — extraordinary investment intensity relative to revenue, reflecting infrastructure-heavy AI cloud model [1].

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (2/4): Nebius's purpose — 'building full-stack infrastructure to service the global AI industry' — is specific and differentiated, with the name combining 'nebula' (cloud) and 'Mobius strip' (infinite possibilities). However, the mission is recent (2024 rebrand) and has not been validated through multi-decade execution [2]. Community Impact (1/2): Nebius's 'sovereign-ready' AI cloud serves EU healthcare and financial institutions with data residency compliance, demonstrating alignment with public interest; the TripleTen edtech subsidiary reskills individuals for technology careers [2]. Employee Retention (1/2): The company has assembled a top-tier leadership team from Silicon Valley and Europe (Marc Boroditsky from Cloudflare as CRO), but the company is less than two years old in its current form, limiting tenure data. Insider Buying (1/2): Founder Arkady Volozh maintains a significant equity stake and personally led the transformation, demonstrating deep conviction. However, insider transaction data shows 16 insider sells and no insider buys in the past year, with ~$15.7M in insider selling over 90 days [3].

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on available metrics. Q1 2026 revenue of $399M (+684% YoY) beat consensus of $391.6M, with adjusted EBITDA of $129.5M (positive for the first time) versus a $53.7M adjusted EBITDA loss in the prior year [1]. Core AI cloud ARR surged 54% QoQ to $1.92B, implying asset productivity of $0.941M per 1 MW. Net income of $621.2M includes a $780.6M equity securities revaluation gain and is not representative of operating profitability — operating margin remains -32.1% [1]. D/E of 132.4% reflects $9.6B in convertible debt against growing equity. Forward PEG of 0.63 is exceptional, reflecting expected massive earnings growth. ROE of 14.1% is nearing the 15% target. The company reiterated 2026 guidance of $3.0-3.4B revenue with 40% adjusted EBITDA margin, while increasing CapEx guidance to $20-25B [1]. Liquidity: $227.81 x 17.8M daily volume = $4.06B daily — passes.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (9/9): Nebius IS the AI infrastructure — the company provides GPU clusters, AI-native cloud platforms, and developer tools that AI companies use to build and run models. AI disruption is structurally impossible for a company whose entire business is enabling AI; increased AI adoption directly increases demand for Nebius's services. The NVIDIA partnership (priority Vera Rubin access), Meta agreement ($12B+ dedicated capacity), and $6.3B in Q1 2026 capital raises all validate Nebius as foundational AI infrastructure [1][2]. AI Opportunity (1/1): 100% of Nebius's revenue comes from AI infrastructure services. Q1 2026 AI cloud revenue grew 684% YoY to $399M with ARR of $1.92B — AI revenue is the entire business [1].

infinite_game.summary: Arkady Volozh's decision to renounce Russian citizenship, sell Yandex's entire Russian business for $5.4B, and rebuild as a Western AI infrastructure company is one of the most audacious acts of existential flexibility in recent business history. The Nebius story is defined by founder-level conviction and sacrifice in service of a genuine — if recently articulated — Just Cause of democratizing AI infrastructure. The company scores well on courage and flexibility but lacks the organizational depth and multi-decade track record for top marks.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): Nebius's purpose — building the infrastructure layer that enables the global AI industry — is authentic, validated by Volozh's extraordinary personal sacrifice (renouncing citizenship, divesting $5.4B in assets), and clearly distinctive from competitors. The name itself ('nebula' + 'Mobius') encodes the infinite aspiration [2]. However, the cause is only two years old and has not been tested through a prolonged downturn or leadership transition — it remains to be proven as a multi-decade organizing principle.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (1/2): The Nebius leadership team has been deliberately assembled from top-tier tech companies (Marc Boroditsky from Cloudflare, John Boynton as Chairman), reflecting a high-caliber talent strategy. The company has grown from a blank slate to a $58B market cap in under two years, requiring extraordinary internal coordination [2]. However, the organization is less than two years old with 1,543 employees — insufficient history to demonstrate long-term team stability or evaluate cultural trust at scale.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): Nebius explicitly positions itself against AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud — not to beat them on scale, but to offer a superior specialized environment for AI training and inference. The company's 'neocloud' positioning acknowledges hyperscaler strengths while carving out a differentiated proposition based on technical depth and AI-native design [2]. This is learning-oriented competitive framing.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (2/2): The transformation from Russia's dominant internet company (Yandex) to a Western AI infrastructure firm is one of the most dramatic strategic pivots in modern business history. Volozh and the board sold everything Russian — $5.4B in assets, decades of accumulated market position, and the founder's own citizenship — to pursue a new vision [2]. This is textbook existential flexibility driven by cause, not desperation — the company chose this path from a position of strength.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): The $20-25B CapEx commitment for 2026 while operating at -32.1% margin, the $6.3B in capital raises including convertible notes, and the 1.2 GW Pennsylvania AI factory investment all represent costly long-term bets made in service of the Just Cause [1]. Volozh's personal sacrifice — renouncing citizenship and rebuilding from scratch — is extraordinary individual courage. However, the company has not yet navigated a downturn or GPU shortage, so the courage remains untested under adversity.

investment_thesis: Nebius is the purest play on AI infrastructure outside of NVIDIA itself — a company growing revenue 684% YoY to $399M in Q1 2026 with core AI cloud ARR of $1.92B (+54% QoQ), validated by NVIDIA ($6.3B in Q1 capital support), Meta ($12B+ infrastructure agreement), and a path to $3.0-3.4B in 2026 revenue with 40% adjusted EBITDA margins [1][2]. The company posted its first positive adjusted EBITDA quarter ($129.5M) while securing 1.2 GW of power for a new Pennsylvania AI factory and priority access to NVIDIA's Vera Rubin platform. With a PEG of 0.63 reflecting massive expected earnings growth, Nebius is among the most attractively priced AI infrastructure assets relative to its growth trajectory.
thesis_continued: The bear case centers on capital intensity and dilution. Nebius is guiding for $20-25B in 2026 CapEx while operating at -32.1% margin, requiring continuous capital raises that dilute shareholders — the $6.3B raised in Q1 alone (convertible notes and NVIDIA equity) demonstrates the pace of dilution [1]. The Forward P/E of 630x on trailing fundamentals reflects speculative pricing for a company with 18 months of operating history in its current form. Customer concentration is extreme — Meta and a handful of large customers drive the majority of revenue, and any delay in the Meta contract or NVIDIA GPU shipments could trigger severe volatility. The 52-week range of $41.40 to $278.84 illustrates the stock's speculative character. Insider selling of ~$15.7M over the past 90 days, with no insider buys recorded in the past year, warrants monitoring [3].
key_catalysts: 2026 revenue hitting $3B+ and adjusted EBITDA margin reaching 40% by year-end would validate the business model as self-sustaining and likely trigger institutional re-rating
NVIDIA Vera Rubin platform deployment in H2 2026 — Nebius will be among the first providers globally, creating a competitive hardware advantage and customer pipeline for 2027
Meta capacity delivery beginning early 2027 — successful execution of the $12B+ agreement would establish Nebius as a tier-one AI infrastructure partner for hyperscalers
key_risks: Capital structure risk: $20-25B 2026 CapEx against $399M quarterly revenue requires continuous dilutive capital raises — any disruption in capital markets access could force CapEx reductions that undermine the growth trajectory

Customer concentration: Meta agreement and NVIDIA relationship represent massive client concentration — loss, delay, or renegotiation of either would materially impair revenue guidance and investor confidence

Operating loss persistence: despite first positive EBITDA quarter, the -32.1% operating margin and 18-month operating history mean profitability at scale remains unproven — any GPU supply chain disruption or pricing pressure could extend the path to breakeven
